facts.text: The largemouth bass is the most popular game fish in North America, pursued by millions of anglers each year.
Fly fishing dates back to at least the 2nd century, when Roman author Claudius Aelianus described Macedonian anglers using artificial flies.
The Bassmaster Classic is often called the Super Bowl of professional bass fishing and has been held annually since 1971.
A spinning reel is the most popular type of fishing reel for beginners because it is easy to cast and less prone to backlash.
The world record largemouth bass weighed 22 pounds 4 ounces and was caught by George Perry in Montgomery Lake, Georgia in 1932.
Rainbow trout get their name from the distinctive pink or red stripe that runs along their lateral line.
The Palomar knot is considered one of the strongest and most reliable fishing knots, retaining nearly 100% of line strength when tied correctly.
Catch and release fishing was popularized in the 1950s and has become a cornerstone of modern fish conservation.
Deep sea fishing typically takes place in waters that are at least 100 feet deep, often miles offshore.
Kevin VanDam, known as KVD, has won the Bassmaster Classic four times and is widely considered the greatest professional bass angler of all time.
The improved clinch knot is one of the most commonly used fishing knots for attaching a hook, lure, or swivel to the fishing line.
Walleye are named for their large, reflective eyes, which give them excellent vision in low-light conditions.
Braided fishing line has virtually no stretch, making it extremely sensitive to bites but also less forgiving on hook sets.
The muskie, or muskellunge, is often called the fish of 10,000 casts due to its difficulty to catch.
Trolling is a fishing technique where lures or baited lines are drawn through the water behind a moving boat.
Ice fishing is practiced extensively in northern states and Canada, where anglers drill holes through frozen lakes to fish below the ice.
The bluegill is one of the most common panfish in North America and is often the first fish a young angler catches.
Crankbaits are hard-bodied lures designed to dive to specific depths when retrieved, imitating baitfish or crawfish.
Bill Dance is a legendary bass fishing personality who has hosted a popular fishing television show since 1968.
Fluorocarbon fishing line is nearly invisible underwater because its refractive index is close to that of water.
The Atlantic salmon is known as the king of fish and has been prized by anglers for centuries in rivers across Europe and North America.
Spinnerbaits feature one or more metal blades that spin like a propeller, creating vibration and flash to attract fish.
Noodling is a form of hand fishing where anglers reach into underwater holes to catch catfish barehanded.
The IGFA (International Game Fish Association) maintains world records for both freshwater and saltwater fish species.
Fly tying is the craft of creating artificial flies used in fly fishing, using materials like feathers, fur, thread, and tinsel.
A baitcasting reel sits on top of the rod and offers greater accuracy and power but requires more skill to prevent backlash.
The smallmouth bass is known for its aggressive fighting ability and acrobatic leaps when hooked.
Soft plastic lures, such as worms and creature baits, are among the most versatile and widely used bass fishing baits.
The brown trout was originally native to Europe and was introduced to North American waters in the 1880s.
Roland Martin holds the record for the most Bassmaster tournament wins with 19 career victories.
Pike are ambush predators with razor-sharp teeth and can strike at speeds of up to 25 miles per hour.
A fish finder uses sonar technology to detect fish and underwater structure beneath the boat.
Surf fishing involves casting from the shoreline or wading into the ocean to catch fish in the surf zone.
The blood knot is primarily used for joining two pieces of fishing line of similar diameter, especially in fly fishing leader construction.
Topwater lures create surface disturbance that mimics struggling prey, producing explosive strikes from predatory fish.
Crappie are among the most popular panfish species and are known for their excellent taste.
The FLW Tour, now known as the Tackle Warehouse Pro Circuit, is one of the premier professional bass fishing tournament trails.
Dry flies are designed to float on the surface of the water, imitating adult insects that have landed on the water.
The world record blue catfish weighed 143 pounds and was caught in Kerr Lake, Virginia in 2011.
Mahi-mahi, also known as dolphinfish, are one of the fastest-growing fish in the ocean and are prized for their acrobatic fights.
Jigging is a technique that involves moving a jig lure up and down in a vertical motion to mimic an injured baitfish.
The loop knot allows a lure to swing freely, giving it more natural action in the water.
Tarpon are known as the silver king and can grow over 200 pounds. They are famous for their spectacular aerial displays when hooked.
Bass fishing tournaments often use a catch-weigh-release format to promote conservation while maintaining competitive angling.
Monofilament fishing line stretches under pressure, which can be an advantage when fighting strong fish as it acts as a shock absorber.
The steelhead is an anadromous form of rainbow trout that migrates from freshwater to the ocean and back to spawn.
Mike Iaconelli, known as Ike, is famous for his energetic personality and won the Bassmaster Classic in 2003.
Buzzbaits are topwater lures with a propeller-like blade that churns the surface, creating noise and vibration to attract bass.
The world record marlin, a black marlin, weighed 1,560 pounds and was caught off Cabo Blanco, Peru in 1953.
Catfish have thousands of taste buds covering their entire body, allowing them to detect food in murky water.
Nymphs are subsurface fly patterns that imitate the larval stage of aquatic insects and account for the majority of a trout's diet.
The Rapala Original Floater, introduced in 1962, is one of the best-selling fishing lures of all time.
Halibut are the largest flatfish in the world and can weigh over 500 pounds. They are a prized catch in Pacific and Atlantic waters.
Circle hooks are designed to hook fish in the corner of the mouth, significantly reducing mortality rates in catch and release fishing.
The Lake Fork Reservoir in Texas has produced more ShareLunker bass (over 13 pounds) than any other lake in the state.
Streamers are fly patterns that imitate baitfish, leeches, or crayfish and are typically fished with an active retrieve.
Carp are one of the most widely distributed freshwater fish in the world and can grow to over 60 pounds.
Hank Parker won the Bassmaster Classic twice, in 1979 and 1989, and went on to host a popular fishing television show.
A tackle box is used to store and organize fishing lures, hooks, sinkers, and other terminal tackle.
Bonefish are one of the most sought-after species for saltwater fly fishing, known for their blistering runs on the flats.
The Texas rig is one of the most popular ways to rig a soft plastic worm, making it weedless for fishing in heavy cover.
Sturgeon are among the oldest fish species on Earth, with fossils dating back over 200 million years.
Red snapper populations in the Gulf of Mexico have been heavily managed through strict fishing regulations and seasonal closures.
The fly reel primarily serves as a line storage device and drag system in fly fishing.
Fishing with live bait such as worms, minnows, and crickets remains one of the most effective methods for catching a wide variety of fish.
The Senko-style soft plastic stickbait, invented by Gary Yamamoto, revolutionized bass fishing with its subtle falling action.
Grouper are bottom-dwelling fish found in warm waters and are known for their powerful runs back toward structure when hooked.
A fish stocking program involves releasing hatchery-raised fish into natural water bodies to supplement wild populations.
Swordfish can swim at speeds up to 60 miles per hour, making them one of the fastest fish in the ocean.
The Carolina rig uses a heavy sinker above a swivel with a long leader to a soft plastic bait, allowing the bait to float off the bottom.
Fly fishing leaders are tapered from a thick butt section to a thin tippet to help turn over the fly during casting.
The jig is considered one of the most versatile fishing lures and can be used for bass, walleye, crappie, and many other species.
Major League Fishing (MLF) uses a catch-weigh-immediate-release format with no limits, changing how professional bass fishing is scored.
The drop shot rig suspends a soft plastic bait above a weight, keeping the lure at a precise depth in the water column.
Salmon runs are annual migrations where salmon swim upstream from the ocean to spawn in the freshwater rivers where they were born.
Night fishing for catfish is extremely popular because catfish are primarily nocturnal feeders that become more active after dark.
The Uni knot, also called the Duncan loop, is a versatile fishing knot that can be used for terminal connections and line-to-line joins.
Bluefin tuna can grow to over 1,000 pounds and are among the most valuable commercial fish in the world.
A fishing rod's power rating indicates its lifting strength, ranging from ultra-light to extra-heavy.
The wacky rig involves hooking a soft plastic worm through the middle, creating an enticing wobbling action as it falls.
Lake Michigan is one of the premier destinations for salmon and trout fishing in the Great Lakes region.
The Alabama rig, also known as an umbrella rig, mimics a school of baitfish using multiple lures on a single frame.
Bottom fishing involves dropping weighted bait to the sea floor to catch species like snapper, grouper, and flounder.
The fly fishing roll cast is used when there is no room for a backcast, such as when fishing near trees or high banks.
Crappie can be caught year-round but are most accessible during spring when they move to shallow water to spawn.
A spinning rod is designed to be used with a spinning reel mounted below the rod, with the line guides facing downward.
The world record walleye weighed 25 pounds and was caught in Old Hickory Lake, Tennessee in 1960.
Swimbaits are realistic-looking lures designed to mimic the swimming action of baitfish, ranging from soft-body to hard-body designs.
The Everglades in Florida is one of the world's premier destinations for largemouth bass and peacock bass fishing.
Fishing licenses are required in all 50 U.S. states and the revenue generated supports fish and wildlife conservation programs.
Tungsten sinkers are denser than lead, allowing anglers to use a smaller sinker that transmits bottom composition better.
The surgeon's knot is a quick and easy knot for joining two lines of different diameters, often used in fly fishing.
Rainbow trout are one of the most stocked fish species in North America, with millions released into lakes and streams annually.
Chatterbaits, also known as bladed jigs, combine a vibrating blade with a jig head and skirt for a unique presentation.
The art of reading water—understanding currents, depth changes, and structure—is essential for successful river fishing.
Catfish stink baits are designed to produce a strong odor that attracts catfish from long distances in murky water.
Baitcasting reels have a spool that rotates during the cast, requiring the angler to use thumb pressure to control line release.
The weight-forward fly line is the most common fly line taper, designed to load the rod quickly and deliver flies at moderate distances.
Kayak fishing has grown enormously in popularity, offering anglers a quiet, affordable way to access remote fishing spots.
Speckled trout, also known as spotted seatrout, are one of the most popular inshore saltwater game fish along the Gulf Coast.
A landing net helps anglers safely land and handle fish, reducing the risk of injury to both the fish and the angler.
The world record smallmouth bass weighed 11 pounds 15 ounces and was caught in Dale Hollow Lake, Kentucky in 1955.
Popping corks are used in saltwater fishing to create noise that attracts redfish, speckled trout, and other inshore species.
Trout tend to face upstream in moving water, waiting for food to drift toward them with the current.
The slip bobber rig allows anglers to fish at any depth while still being able to cast easily.
Peacock bass, native to South America, have been introduced to South Florida and are known for their aggressive strikes.
Fluorocarbon leader material is popular in saltwater fishing because of its abrasion resistance and near-invisibility underwater.
The Heddon Zara Spook is a legendary topwater lure that produces a walk-the-dog action when worked with rhythmic rod twitches.
Bass spawn in spring when water temperatures reach approximately 60 to 70 degrees Fahrenheit.
A gaff is a large hook on a pole used to land heavy fish, commonly used in saltwater fishing.
The fly fishing double haul is a casting technique that increases line speed and distance by pulling on the line during both the forward and back casts.
Walleye are considered one of the best-tasting freshwater fish due to their mild, flaky white flesh.
The Ned rig, a simple finesse presentation using a small mushroom jig head and trimmed soft plastic, has become a dominant bass fishing technique.
Fishing with artificial lures requires more skill and technique but allows anglers to cover water more efficiently than live bait.
The world record channel catfish weighed 58 pounds and was caught in Santee Cooper Reservoir, South Carolina in 1964.
A downrigger is a device used in trolling to maintain lures at a precise depth using a heavy weight on a cable.
Striped bass are anadromous fish that migrate from saltwater to freshwater rivers to spawn each spring.
The fly fishing mend is a technique used to reposition the fly line on the water to achieve a drag-free drift.
Jerkbaits are minnow-shaped lures that are fished with sharp rod twitches to imitate a wounded baitfish.
The Great Barrier Reef off Australia is one of the world's premier destinations for saltwater sport fishing.
Catch and release mortality can be significantly reduced by using barbless hooks, handling fish with wet hands, and minimizing air exposure.
The Abu Garcia Ambassadeur, introduced in 1952, was one of the first round baitcasting reels and remains popular today.
Fly fishing for bonefish on tropical flats is often called flats fishing and requires sight-casting to individual fish.
Fishing pressure refers to the number of anglers fishing a particular body of water, which can affect fish behavior and catch rates.
A fish's lateral line is a sensory organ that detects vibrations and pressure changes in the water, helping fish locate prey and avoid predators.
The shaky head is a finesse bass fishing technique that involves slowly dragging a worm on a special jig head along the bottom.
Muskie fishing often requires specialized heavy tackle, large lures, and enormous patience due to the fish's rarity and size.
Fishing with a bobber, or float, suspends bait at a set depth and provides a visual indicator when a fish bites.
The Rio Grande is a famous fly fishing destination for trout in New Mexico and Colorado.
A fishing rod's action describes where the rod flexes—fast action rods bend near the tip, while moderate rods flex through the middle.
Chumming involves dispersing bait into the water to attract fish to a specific area, a technique commonly used in saltwater fishing.
The world record rainbow trout weighed 48 pounds and was caught in Lake Diefenbaker, Saskatchewan in 2009.
Sabiki rigs feature multiple small hooks and are used to catch baitfish such as pilchards and minnows.
Largemouth bass prefer warm, weedy water with plenty of cover such as lily pads, submerged timber, and docks.
The Woolly Bugger is one of the most versatile and effective fly patterns, capable of catching trout, bass, and panfish.
Tenkara is a Japanese form of fly fishing that uses a long, telescopic rod with no reel, relying solely on a fixed line and fly.
The Kenai River in Alaska is world-famous for its king salmon runs, with fish regularly exceeding 50 pounds.
Soft plastic crawfish imitations are among the best baits for bass, as crawfish are a primary food source in many lakes.
Barbless hooks make catch and release easier and reduce handling time, improving fish survival rates.
The Shimano Stella is widely regarded as one of the finest spinning reels ever manufactured.
Pitching and flipping are short-distance casting techniques used to present lures quietly into heavy cover.
Lake Okeechobee in Florida is one of the top largemouth bass fisheries in the United States.
The Pere Marquette River in Michigan is one of the premier fly fishing rivers for steelhead in the Midwest.
A fish scale or digital scale is used to weigh caught fish accurately during tournaments and for record-keeping.
The Snell knot provides a straight pull from the line through the eye and along the shank of the hook for a better hookup ratio.
Largemouth bass have a large mouth that extends past the eye, while smallmouth bass have a mouth that does not extend beyond the eye.
Fishing during the golden hours—dawn and dusk—is often the most productive time because fish are actively feeding.
The Berkeley Gulp! line of soft plastic baits uses scent-infused biodegradable material that attracts fish through their sense of smell.
Pier fishing is a popular and accessible form of saltwater fishing that requires no boat.
The San Juan Worm is a simple fly pattern that imitates aquatic worms and is extremely effective for trout in tailwaters.
Fishing with a slip sinker allows a fish to take the bait without feeling the weight of the sinker.
The Amazon River basin is home to more fish species than any other river system in the world, including the giant arapaima.
A rod holder allows anglers to fish multiple rods simultaneously while trolling or still fishing.
The Clouser Minnow, designed by Bob Clouser, is one of the most effective saltwater fly patterns ever created.
Spawn sacks containing fish eggs are a highly effective bait for steelhead and salmon in Great Lakes tributaries.
Electronics like GPS-enabled fish finders have revolutionized modern fishing by allowing precise navigation and fish location.
The world record muskie weighed 67 pounds 8 ounces and was caught in Lac Courte Oreilles, Wisconsin in 1949.
Power fishing involves using reaction baits like crankbaits and spinnerbaits to cover water quickly and trigger reflexive strikes.
The Royal Wulff is a classic dry fly pattern that has been catching trout since its creation by Lee Wulff in the 1930s.
Fishing regulations vary by state and body of water, including limits on daily catch, minimum size, and seasonal restrictions.
A leader is a separate section of line between the main line and the hook or lure, often used for its abrasion resistance or invisibility.
The Yellowstone River is the longest undammed river in the lower 48 states and is a premier trout fishing destination.
Suspending jerkbaits are designed to hover at a specific depth when paused, imitating a dying baitfish.
Flathead catfish are solitary predators that prefer live bait and can grow to over 100 pounds.
The world record pike weighed 55 pounds 1 ounce and was caught in Greffern Lake, Germany in 1986.
A trolling motor provides quiet, electric propulsion that allows anglers to move through fishing spots without spooking fish.
Fly fishing tippet is the thin, nearly invisible section of line at the end of the leader that connects to the fly.
The Rebel Pop-R is a classic topwater popper that has been catching bass for decades with its cupped face and spitting action.
Fishing with cut bait—pieces of fresh fish—is one of the most effective methods for catching catfish and other bottom feeders.
The Madison River in Montana is considered one of the best trout streams in the world, offering exceptional dry fly fishing.
A fish grip tool allows anglers to safely hold fish by the jaw without harming the fish or the angler.
The Elk Hair Caddis is a popular dry fly that imitates adult caddisflies and floats well in rough water.
Crappie jigs tipped with small soft plastics or live minnows are the most popular method for catching crappie.
The Colorado River offers diverse fishing opportunities from cold-water trout fishing below dams to warm-water bass fishing in reservoirs.
A polarized pair of sunglasses is one of the most important pieces of fishing gear, cutting surface glare so anglers can see fish and structure beneath the water.
Fishing tournaments typically have strict rules about the number and size of fish that can be weighed, with penalties for dead fish.
The world record brown trout weighed 44 pounds 11 ounces and was caught in the Ohau Canal, New Zealand in 2020.
A swimbait is a type of fishing lure designed to replicate the natural swimming motion of a baitfish.
The Copper John is a popular beadhead nymph fly pattern known for its effectiveness in catching trout year-round.
Fishing with live minnows under a bobber is one of the most effective techniques for catching crappie, walleye, and bass.
The White River in Arkansas is one of the most productive trout fisheries in the country, thanks to the cold water released from Bull Shoals Dam.
Umbrella rigs were controversial when first used in bass tournaments due to their ability to present multiple lures simultaneously.
Largemouth bass have been introduced to countries around the world, including Japan, where bass fishing has a massive following.
A fly box is a specialized storage case designed to organize and protect artificial flies for fly fishing.
Trolling for salmon in the Great Lakes is a popular technique that involves running multiple rods at different depths behind a boat.
The world record yellowfin tuna weighed 427 pounds and was caught near Cabo San Lucas, Mexico in 2012.
Finesse fishing uses lighter tackle, smaller baits, and subtle presentations to catch fish that are not actively feeding.
Carp fishing is enormously popular in Europe, where specialized techniques like hair rigs and boilies are standard.
The Green Pumpkin color in soft plastic lures is one of the most universally effective colors for bass fishing.
Striped bass, also called stripers or rockfish, are one of the most popular game fish on the East Coast of the United States.
A fishing kayak is typically sit-on-top design with built-in rod holders, gear storage, and sometimes pedal drive systems.
The Prince Nymph is a versatile subsurface fly that imitates a variety of aquatic insects with its peacock herl body and white biots.
Ice fishing shanties, also called ice houses, provide shelter from wind and cold while fishing through holes in the ice.
The Snook is a popular inshore game fish found in tropical and subtropical waters, known for its explosive strikes and acrobatic fights.
Lead-free fishing tackle has been promoted to reduce lead poisoning in waterfowl and other wildlife that ingest lost sinkers.
A tip-up is an ice fishing device that signals when a fish takes the bait by raising a flag.
The Bighorn River in Montana is known for its high density of large brown and rainbow trout per mile.
Frog lures are topwater baits designed to be fished over dense vegetation like lily pads and mats where bass hide.
Night fishing with black lights illuminates fluorescent fishing line, making it easier to detect bites in the dark.
The world record crappie, a white crappie, weighed 5 pounds 3 ounces and was caught in Enid Dam, Mississippi in 1957.
A fish hook's gap is the distance between the shank and the point, which affects how well it can penetrate a fish's mouth.
The Pheasant Tail Nymph, created by Frank Sawyer, is one of the most effective and widely used nymph patterns in fly fishing.
Bass boats are specialized high-performance watercraft designed for tournament bass fishing, featuring powerful outboard motors and raised casting decks.
The Chesapeake Bay is one of the most productive estuaries in the world, supporting fisheries for striped bass, blue crabs, and more.
A snap swivel allows anglers to quickly change lures without retying knots.
Cutthroat trout are native to western North America and are named for the distinctive red or orange slash marks under their jaw.
The Dardevle spoon is one of the oldest and most iconic fishing lures, first manufactured in 1906.
Trophy bass fishing culture values catching and photographing large fish before releasing them to be caught again.
The world record flathead catfish weighed 123 pounds and was caught in Elk City Reservoir, Kansas in 1998.
A fishing vest is designed with multiple pockets to carry fly boxes, tippet, leaders, and other small gear for wade fishing.
The Columbia River system in the Pacific Northwest supports massive salmon and steelhead runs each year.
Lipless crankbaits, also called rattle traps, produce a tight vibrating action and work well in shallow water and over submerged grass.
The world record striped bass in freshwater weighed 67 pounds 8 ounces and was caught in O'Neill Forebay, California in 1992.
A fly fishing strike indicator is essentially a float that helps anglers detect subtle takes when nymph fishing.
Fishing line memory refers to the tendency of monofilament line to retain the shape of the spool, causing coils that reduce casting distance.
The Florida strain largemouth bass grows larger than its northern counterpart due to a longer growing season and warmer water.
Punching is a technique used to penetrate thick mats of vegetation with a heavy tungsten weight and a soft plastic bait.
The Deschutes River in Oregon is a legendary fly fishing destination known for its wild redbands and summer steelhead.
Fishing with circle hooks has been mandated in some fisheries because they dramatically reduce deep hooking and fish mortality.
The world record sockeye salmon weighed 15 pounds 3 ounces and was caught in the Kenai River, Alaska in 1987.
Rod sensitivity is crucial in bass fishing; graphite rods transmit vibrations from the line to the angler's hand, allowing detection of subtle bites.
Saltwater fly fishing for tarpon in the Florida Keys is considered one of the ultimate challenges in sport fishing.
A wading staff provides stability and safety when wading in rivers with strong currents or slippery bottoms.
The Adams is a classic dry fly pattern that imitates a wide range of mayflies and is effective on trout streams worldwide.
Fishing with braided line requires a fluorocarbon or monofilament leader in clear water because braid is highly visible to fish.
The Susquehanna River in Pennsylvania is one of the best smallmouth bass fisheries on the East Coast.
A planer board is a device used in trolling that pulls the fishing line away from the boat, allowing anglers to cover a wider area.
The world record largemouth bass was tied in 2009 when Manabu Kurita caught a 22-pound 4-ounce bass in Lake Biwa, Japan.
Fishing in current seams—where fast and slow water meet—is a prime strategy for locating trout in rivers.
The Gulp! Alive series stores soft plastic baits in attractant-filled jars for maximum scent dispersion underwater.
An aerated livewell keeps caught fish alive during tournaments by circulating fresh, oxygenated water.
The Au Sable River in Michigan is considered the birthplace of American fly fishing and is home to the famed Adams fly pattern.
Fishing with a Texas-rigged worm is one of the most effective methods for catching bass in heavy cover like brush and timber.
Bowfishing uses a specialized bow and arrow to shoot fish, typically targeting carp, gar, and other rough fish in shallow water.
The Stimulator is a large, bushy dry fly that imitates stoneflies and grasshoppers and works well on western trout streams.
Night crawlers are one of the most universally effective fishing baits, appealing to bass, trout, catfish, walleye, and many other species.
The Lake of the Ozarks in Missouri is a popular bass fishing destination with over 1,100 miles of shoreline.
Treble hooks have three points and are commonly found on crankbaits, jerkbaits, and topwater lures.
The world record Pacific halibut weighed 459 pounds and was caught near Unalaska, Alaska in 1996.
Fishing with a drift boat allows anglers to float rivers while making precise casts to holding water along the banks.
The Hare's Ear Nymph is a fly pattern that imitates a variety of aquatic insect larvae and is effective on streams worldwide.
A fish passage, or fish ladder, allows migrating fish to pass upstream over dams and other barriers.
The Flipping Stick is a heavy-action rod designed specifically for pitching and flipping into heavy cover.
The Bristol Bay region of Alaska produces the largest sockeye salmon run in the world, supporting both commercial and sport fisheries.
A hook remover or hemostats make it easier to safely remove hooks from fish, reducing handling time and injury.
The Deceiver, designed by Lefty Kreh, is one of the most popular and effective saltwater fly patterns.
Carp fishing in North America has gained popularity with the spread of European-style fishing techniques.
The world record mahi-mahi weighed 87 pounds and was caught off Costa Rica in 1976.
Seasonal patterns affect fish behavior; bass move shallow in spring to spawn and deep in summer to find cooler water.
Fishing from a belly boat, or float tube, allows anglers to access remote areas of lakes that are difficult to reach by foot or boat.
The Zonker is a streamer fly pattern that uses a strip of rabbit fur for the wing, giving it lifelike movement in the water.
Fishing with PowerBait, a scented and flavored dough bait, is one of the most popular methods for catching stocked trout.
The Kern River in California offers excellent fly fishing for wild rainbow trout in a stunning mountain setting.
Lead core line is used in trolling to achieve specific depths without the need for a downrigger.
The Bassmaster Angler of the Year award recognizes the most consistent performer throughout the entire tournament season.
Catfish whiskers, or barbels, contain taste receptors that help the fish locate food in dark or murky conditions.
The world record brook trout weighed 14 pounds 8 ounces and was caught in the Nipigon River, Ontario in 1915.
Fishing with a weedless spoon through heavy vegetation is an effective way to target bass hiding in thick cover.
The Gallatin River in Montana offers excellent walk-and-wade fly fishing for brown and rainbow trout.
A fish attractant spray or gel can be applied to artificial lures to add scent and increase bites.
Party boats, also called head boats, offer affordable offshore fishing trips where seats are sold individually.
The Parachute Adams is a variation of the Adams dry fly with a white wing post that makes it highly visible on the water.
The world record peacock bass weighed 29 pounds and was caught in the Rio Negro, Brazil in 2010.
Fishing with a crankbait that contacts the bottom and deflects off rocks and stumps creates an erratic action that triggers reaction strikes.
The South Fork of the Snake River in Idaho is one of the best cutthroat trout fisheries in the West.
A rod locker on a bass boat provides secure storage for fishing rods during travel between fishing spots.
The Muddler Minnow, created by Don Gapen, imitates sculpin and other bottom-dwelling baitfish in fly fishing.
Bank fishing requires no boat and is one of the most accessible forms of fishing, available at ponds, lakes, rivers, and reservoirs.
Bucktail jigs are effective for a wide range of saltwater species including striped bass, fluke, and bluefish.
The Henry's Fork of the Snake River in Idaho is famous for its extremely challenging and technical dry fly fishing.
Fishing with live shrimp is one of the most effective methods for catching redfish, snook, and trout in saltwater.
A stringer is a device used to keep caught fish alive in the water while continuing to fish.
The world record blue marlin weighed 1,805 pounds and was caught off Vitoria, Brazil in 1992.
Sight fishing involves spotting fish in shallow, clear water and making targeted casts to individual fish.
The Rogue River in Oregon is a nationally recognized destination for steelhead and salmon fishing.
A fishing rod blank is the unfinished shaft from which a complete rod is built by adding guides, a reel seat, and a handle.
The Crazy Charlie is a bonefish fly pattern that has accounted for more bonefish catches than perhaps any other fly.
Fishing with a drop shot rig is particularly effective for finicky bass in deep or clear water conditions.
The Dale Hollow Reservoir on the Tennessee-Kentucky border holds the world record for smallmouth bass.
Fishing with a spoon lure, one of the oldest artificial lures, imitates a wounded baitfish with its wobbling, flashing action.
Tidal movements significantly affect saltwater fishing, as incoming tides bring baitfish into estuaries and outgoing tides concentrate them.
The CDC (cul de canard) feather from near a duck's preen gland is a prized fly tying material because of its natural buoyancy.
The world record striper caught in saltwater weighed 81 pounds 14 ounces, taken from Long Island Sound in 2011.
Fishing from a pontoon boat offers a stable, comfortable platform suitable for both recreation and serious fishing.
The Thingamabobber is a popular fly fishing strike indicator known for its round shape and high visibility.
The St. Johns River in Florida is one of the few rivers in the United States that flows north and offers excellent bass fishing.
A depthfinder or graph shows the bottom contour beneath the boat, helping anglers identify drop-offs, humps, and channels where fish congregate.
Fishing with blade baits is a highly effective technique for walleye and smallmouth bass, especially in cold water.
The Battenkill River in Vermont is one of the most famous trout streams in the northeastern United States.
A Penn reel is an iconic saltwater fishing reel brand that has been manufacturing reels since 1932.
The world record carp weighed over 100 pounds and was caught in France, where carp fishing is a premier sport.
Fishing with a worm harness, a series of spinner blades ahead of a baited hook, is one of the best techniques for catching walleye.
Fishing in tailraces below dams can produce exceptional catches because the current concentrates baitfish and attracts predators.
The BWO (Blue-Winged Olive) is a mayfly species that hatches prolifically in overcast and rainy conditions, creating outstanding dry fly fishing.
Fishing guides provide expert local knowledge, equipment, and instruction, making guided trips valuable for both beginners and advanced anglers.
The world record king salmon, also known as Chinook, weighed 97 pounds 4 ounces and was caught in the Kenai River, Alaska in 1985.
A hawg trough is a measuring device used in bass tournaments to quickly verify that fish meet minimum length requirements.
Fishing with a jig and pig—a jig tipped with a pork or plastic chunk trailer—is a classic big bass technique.
The Delaware River provides excellent smallmouth bass and trout fishing close to major population centers on the East Coast.
Fishing during a barometric pressure change—especially a falling barometer—can trigger increased feeding activity in many fish species.
The Stimulator fly pattern in larger sizes is an excellent grasshopper imitation for summer fly fishing.
Fishing with a tandem rig, using two lures or flies at once, increases the chances of finding what fish are feeding on.
The world record white sturgeon caught on rod and reel weighed 468 pounds and was taken from the Fraser River in British Columbia.
Livescoping uses forward-facing sonar to show real-time images of fish and structure, revolutionizing modern bass fishing.
The San Juan River in New Mexico is a legendary tailwater fishery known for large rainbow and brown trout.
Fishing with topwater lures in the early morning can produce heart-stopping surface strikes from bass and other predators.
A leader wallet organizes pre-tied leaders by length and tippet size for quick access while fly fishing.
The Yakima River in Washington is a productive trout fishery that offers opportunities for both wild rainbow and cutthroat trout.
Fishing with a hair jig—a jig tied with natural or synthetic hair—is a traditional technique for smallmouth bass and walleye.
The world record Atlantic salmon caught on a fly weighed 79 pounds 2 ounces and came from Norway's Tana River in 1928.
A fish cleaning station provides a dedicated area with running water for filleting and processing caught fish.
The Chernobyl Ant is a large foam fly that imitates terrestrial insects and excels as a dry fly indicator in hopper-dropper rigs.
Fishing for permit on tropical flats is considered one of the most difficult challenges in all of fly fishing.
The Sacramento River in California is an important salmon and steelhead fishery on the West Coast.
A hookset is the action of pulling the rod to drive the hook point into the fish's mouth, and timing varies by species and technique.
Inline spinners like the Mepps and Rooster Tail are simple, effective lures for trout, bass, and panfish.
The world record bluegill weighed 4 pounds 12 ounces and was caught in Ketona Lake, Alabama in 1950.
Fishing in oxbow lakes—crescent-shaped bodies of water formed by old river channels—can produce outstanding bass fishing.
The Lefty's Deceiver fly pattern is versatile enough to catch everything from striped bass to tarpon to snook.
Fishing tournaments have grown into a multi-million dollar industry with professional anglers earning sponsorships and prize money.
The Test River in England is considered the birthplace of modern fly fishing and dry fly techniques.
A snelled hook has the line or leader tied in a specific way that runs along the hook shank, providing a straight pull.
Fishing with a bobber and worm is one of the simplest and most effective methods for introducing children to fishing.
The world record tarpon caught on fly tackle weighed 202 pounds 8 ounces and was caught in the Florida Keys.
A fillet knife with a thin, flexible blade is essential for properly cleaning and filleting fish.
The Provo River in Utah is one of the most productive trout streams in the West, with significant brown trout populations.
Fishing with a Carolina rig is most effective on hard, clean bottoms where the heavy sinker can drag and create noise.
The Green River in Utah below Flaming Gorge Dam is known for its incredible population of large brown and rainbow trout.
A PFD (personal flotation device) is essential safety equipment for any angler fishing from a boat or kayak.
The Zebra Midge is a tiny but extremely effective fly for trout, imitating chironomid larvae in stillwater and tailwaters.
Fishing with plastic worms has been popular since Nick Creme invented the first soft plastic fishing lure in 1949.
The Outer Banks of North Carolina offer diverse fishing opportunities from surf fishing to offshore blue marlin.
A rod tube protects fishing rods during travel and storage, preventing damage to guides and blanks.
The Bivisible is a classic dry fly designed to be visible to both the angler and the trout.
Fishing with live crawfish as bait is one of the most effective methods for catching large smallmouth bass.
The Miramichi River in New Brunswick, Canada is one of the premier Atlantic salmon rivers in the world.
A seine net is used to catch baitfish for use as live bait in both freshwater and saltwater fishing.
The world record permit weighed 60 pounds and was caught off Fort Lauderdale, Florida in 2002.
Fishing log books help anglers track patterns in their catches including weather conditions, water temperature, and successful techniques.
The X-Rap by Rapala is a modern jerkbait designed with internal weight transfer for long casting distance.
Fishing from bridges can provide access to deep water structure and current breaks that hold fish in both fresh and saltwater.
The Bow River in Calgary, Alberta is one of the best urban trout fisheries in the world.
A planer or diving device pulls the fishing line down to a specific depth when trolling, eliminating the need for heavy weights.
The world record bonefish weighed 16 pounds and was caught in Zululand, South Africa in 1962.
Fishing with scented soft plastics has been shown to increase the duration that bass hold onto the bait before spitting it out.
Dock fishing is a popular technique for bass, as boat docks provide shade and structure that attract fish.
The Missouri River is one of the longest rivers in North America and offers diverse fishing opportunities from trout to walleye.
Wading boots with felt or rubber soles provide traction on slippery river bottoms for safe wade fishing.
The Woolly Worm is one of the simplest wet fly patterns and has been used by anglers for centuries.
Fishing in the rain can increase catch rates because overcast skies reduce light penetration and make fish more active.
The world record wahoo weighed 184 pounds and was caught off Cabo San Lucas, Mexico in 2005.
The world of competitive fly fishing has its own tournament circuits and world championships governed by FIPS-Mouche.
The Jitterbug, made by Arbogast, is a classic topwater lure with a hinged lip that creates a gurgling sound on the surface.
Fishing the thermocline—a layer of water where temperature changes rapidly—can help locate fish in deep lakes during summer.
The Rio Grande cichlid is found in Texas and is an increasingly popular sport fish in the state's urban waterways.
A fish counter or clicker helps tournament anglers keep track of how many legal fish they have caught during competition.
The Griffith's Gnat is a tiny fly pattern used to imitate clusters of midges on the water surface.
Fishing with a float and fly is a cold-water smallmouth bass technique that uses a tiny jig suspended below a float.
The Zambezi River in Africa offers tiger fish angling, one of the most exciting freshwater fishing experiences in the world.
A gimbal belt helps distribute the fighting load of a heavy fish across the angler's body during big game battles.
The Sparkle Pupa is an emerger fly pattern designed to imitate caddisflies transitioning from the pupal to adult stage.
Fishing with a ned rig involves slowly dragging or hopping a small mushroom-head jig tipped with a short piece of soft plastic along the bottom.
The Snake River in Wyoming flows through Grand Teton National Park and provides excellent cutthroat trout fishing.
A fish gripper with a built-in scale allows catch-and-release anglers to quickly weigh and photograph fish before release.
The Gold-Ribbed Hare's Ear is a variation of the classic nymph pattern with gold wire ribbing for added durability and flash.
Fishing for northern pike through the ice using tip-ups baited with live minnows is a time-honored tradition in northern states.
The Cape Cod Canal in Massachusetts is a legendary shore fishing location for striped bass and bluefish.
A rod sock is a thin, mesh-like sleeve that protects rod guides during storage and transport.
The world record tiger muskie weighed 44 pounds 4 ounces and was caught in Lac Vieux-Desert, Michigan in 1919.
Fishing using the pendulum cast technique in surf fishing allows anglers to achieve significantly greater casting distances.
The San Gabriel Mountains in Southern California offer surprisingly good trout fishing just a short drive from Los Angeles.
A chartreuse and white pattern is one of the most effective color combinations for spinnerbaits in murky water.
Fishing catch limits are set by wildlife agencies based on population surveys and biological data to ensure sustainable harvest levels.
The Ausable River in New York's Adirondack Mountains is a famed fly fishing destination for brook and brown trout.
Outriggers extend fishing lines away from the sides of a boat during trolling, preventing tangles and covering more water.
The world record spotted bass weighed 11 pounds 4 ounces and was caught in Pine Flat Lake, California in 2001.
Fishing with a popping bug is one of the most exciting ways to catch bass and panfish on a fly rod.
The Bow Narrows region of Ontario, Canada is renowned for trophy walleye and northern pike fishing.
Belly boats, or float tubes, use inflatable chambers and flippers to allow anglers to fish from small bodies of water.
The Humpy is a high-floating, heavily hackled dry fly that works well in fast, turbulent water.
Fishing with a skirted jig is one of the top techniques for catching bass around wood, rocks, and other hard structure.
The Great Lakes collectively offer some of the finest freshwater fishing in the world for salmon, trout, walleye, and smallmouth bass.
A mesh live bait bag keeps shiners and minnows alive in the water until they are needed for fishing.
The Dave's Hopper is a classic grasshopper fly pattern that is highly effective during summer months on western trout streams.
Fishing with braided line as a main line with a fluorocarbon leader combines sensitivity with invisibility underwater.
The Llano River in the Texas Hill Country is one of the best Guadalupe bass fisheries in the state.
A manual downrigger uses a hand crank to lower and raise a heavy ball that keeps trolling lures at a set depth.
The world record freshwater drum weighed 54 pounds 8 ounces and was caught in Nickajack Lake, Tennessee in 1972.
Kayak bass fishing tournaments have grown into a thriving competitive circuit with national championships.
The October Caddis is a large, late-season caddisfly that produces excellent dry fly fishing on western rivers in fall.
Fishing in farm ponds is a quintessential American pastime and can produce surprisingly large bass in lightly fished waters.
The Calcasieu Lake in Louisiana is one of the top destinations for trophy speckled trout fishing in the Gulf Coast region.
Reel drag systems use washers to apply adjustable pressure to a spool, allowing fish to take line without breaking it.
The Comparadun is a low-riding, no-hackle dry fly designed to imitate mayfly duns with a realistic silhouette.
Fishing with a rubber worm was once controversial among purists but has become one of the most popular and effective bass fishing techniques.
Lake Erie's central basin is called the Walleye Capital of the World due to its incredible walleye populations.
A boga grip is a tool that holds fish securely by the lower jaw for weighing and photographing before release.
The Catskill region of New York is historically significant in American fly fishing and produced many legendary fly patterns and anglers.
Fishing with scuds—small freshwater crustaceans—is extremely effective for trout in spring creeks and tailwaters.
The figure-eight maneuver at boatside is a critical technique in muskie fishing, as muskies often follow lures to the boat before striking.
Fishing with gulp shrimp under a popping cork is one of the most effective techniques for catching speckled trout in the Gulf.
